funding_source: No external funding. Internal research project.
citi_training_completion: Principal Investigator has completed CITI training in Human Subjects Research (Social/Behavioral Research).
protocol_description: This study examines how individuals' interests, values, knowledge, skills, and personality align with occupational profiles from O*NET. Participants compare their profile to 867 occupations and receive fit scores and personalized feedback. We are testing the utility of whole-person fit assessment for career exploration and educational settings.
population_description: Participants will be undergraduate students enrolled in Management and Marketing and related courses at Nicholls State University. Participants must be at least 18 years of age. There are no specific exclusion criteria beyond age requirements. Participants will be recruited through the PRAMS system and may receive course credit for participation.
research_procedures:
The study involves the following procedures:

1. Informed Consent: Participants will read and provide electronic consent before beginning the study.

2. Demographics and Background: Participants provide basic demographic information.

3. Whole-Person Fit Assessment: Participants complete the online assessment comparing their interests, values, knowledge, skills, and personality to 867 O*NET occupational profiles. They receive fit scores and personalized feedback.

4. Debriefing: Participants receive information about the study purpose, occupational fit concepts, and resources for further exploration.

All data collection will occur online through the servers hosted by Nicholls State University. The entire procedure takes approximately 15–20 minutes to complete.
research_objectives:
The primary objectives of this research are:

1. To measure how individuals' profiles align with occupational requirements across O*NET occupations
2. To provide participants with feedback on occupational fit for career exploration
3. To contribute to understanding of whole-person fit assessment in educational and career development contexts
4. To support students' career decision-making through evidence-based feedback
research_questions:
1. How do participants' interests, values, knowledge, skills, and personality align with different occupational profiles?
2. Which occupations show the highest fit scores for typical participant profiles?
3. Does whole-person fit feedback influence participants' career exploration or decision-making?
educational_justification:
This research serves educational purposes by:
- Providing students with hands-on experience in career assessment and occupational fit
- Teaching students about O*NET and evidence-based career exploration
- Demonstrating research methodology and data collection procedures
- Contributing to students' understanding of career development and occupational fit
recruitment_method: Participants will be recruited through the Participant Recruitment and Management System (PRAMS) platform. Students enrolled at Nicholls State University will be able to view and sign up for this study through the PRAMS system. Recruitment materials will be posted on the PRAMS system. Announcements may also be made through word of mouth and during Career Week.
recruitment_script:
Study Title: Whole-Person Fit Assessment

Description: Compare your interests, values, knowledge, skills, and personality to 867 O*NET occupations. Get fit scores and personalized feedback.

Duration: Approximately 15–20 minutes
Potential Credit Value: 1 credit (to participating courses)
Mode: Online (complete at your own pace)

Requirements: Must be 18 years of age or older
inclusion_criteria:
- Must be at least 18 years of age
- Must be currently enrolled as a student at Nicholls State University
- Must have access to a computer or device with internet connection
- Must be able to complete online assessments
exclusion_criteria:
- Under 18 years of age
- Not currently enrolled at Nicholls State University
- Unable to complete online assessments due to technical limitations
- Previously participated in this study (to avoid duplicate responses)
benefits_to_subjects:
Participants will benefit from:
- Potentially receiving course credit for participation (1 credit)
- Gaining insight into their occupational fit through immediate feedback
- Learning about career exploration and O*NET through the debriefing
- Contributing to scientific understanding of whole-person fit assessment
benefits_to_others:
This research may benefit:
- Other students and researchers interested in career assessment
- Educators developing career development programs
- Organizations seeking to understand occupational fit and career exploration
benefits_to_society:
This research contributes to:
- Scientific understanding of whole-person fit as a career assessment approach
- Understanding of occupational fit in educational settings
- Development of better career assessment and guidance methods
- Educational practices related to career development
payment_compensation: Participants may receive 1 course credit through the PRAMS system for their participation. No monetary compensation will be provided. Feedback reports are made available.
costs_to_subjects: The only cost to participants is their time (approximately 15–20 minutes). There are no financial costs, physical risks, or other burdens associated with participation.
review_type_justification:
This study qualifies for exempt status under Category D (Educational settings, anonymous surveys/interviews) because:

1. The study involves minimal risk to participants (online assessments)
2. All data collection is anonymous - participants are identified only by PRAMS ID numbers
3. The study involves standard educational and career assessment procedures
4. No sensitive topics or vulnerable populations are involved
5. Participants are adults (18+) who can provide informed consent
6. The study does not involve deception, physical procedures, or collection of sensitive information
7. All procedures are standard in educational research settings

The study involves only anonymous data collection through online assessments, with no collection of identifying information beyond PRAMS ID numbers (which are already anonymized in the system).
risk_statement:
This study involves minimal risk to participants. The primary potential risks are:

1. Psychological discomfort: Feedback may differ from expectations. However, all feedback is provided in a supportive, educational context with emphasis on learning and career exploration.

2. Time commitment: Participants invest approximately 15–20 minutes of their time, but this may be compensated with course credit.

3. Privacy concerns: While all data is anonymized, there is a minimal risk of data breach. However, no personally identifying information is collected beyond PRAMS ID numbers.

The risks in this study are no greater than those encountered in normal daily activities or routine educational assessments.
risk_mitigation:
Risks are mitigated through:

1. Comprehensive debriefing: All participants receive debriefing explaining the study purpose and occupational fit concepts in a supportive, educational context.

2. Anonymization: All data is collected with PRAMS ID numbers only - no names, email addresses, or other identifying information is collected.

3. Voluntary participation: Participants can withdraw at any time without penalty.

4. Secure data storage: All data is stored securely on password-protected servers with access limited to the research team.

5. Support resources: Participants are provided with resources for further career exploration if they are interested.
data_collection_methods:
Data will be collected through the PRAMS online platform using the following methods:

1. Demographic and background information: Collected through online forms
2. Whole-Person Fit Assessment: Collected through web-based assessment interface
3. Feedback responses: Collected through online forms

All data collection occurs through secure, encrypted online forms. Data is automatically stored in the PRAMS system database with PRAMS ID numbers as the only identifier.
data_storage: All data will be stored securely on password-protected servers maintained by Nicholls State University. Data will be stored in the PRAMS system database, which uses industry-standard encryption and security measures. Access to data is restricted to the principal investigator and authorized research team members. Data will be stored with PRAMS ID numbers only - no personally identifying information will be stored with the research data.
confidentiality_procedures:
Confidentiality is maintained through:

1. Anonymization: Participants are identified only by PRAMS ID numbers, which are already anonymized in the PRAMS system.

2. Secure access: Only the principal investigator and authorized research team members have access to the data.

3. Data encryption: All data is stored using industry-standard encryption methods.

4. Limited data sharing: Data will only be shared in aggregated, anonymized form for publication purposes. Individual participant data will be shared but in anonymized form.

5. IRB compliance: All procedures follow IRB guidelines for data confidentiality and security.
data_retention: Research data will be retained for a minimum of 5 years after study completion, as required by IRB regulations. After this period, data may be retained longer for potential follow-up analyses or publication purposes. Anonymized data/analyses will be made available to the Open Science Framework.
data_access:
Access to research data is restricted to:
- Principal Investigator (Dr. Christopher Castille)
- Authorized research team members (if any)
- IRB members for review purposes (if requested)

Data will be accessed only for research analysis, publication preparation, and IRB compliance.
consent_procedures:
Informed consent will be obtained electronically through the PRAMS system before participants begin the study. The consent process includes:

1. Consent form display: Participants will see a comprehensive consent form explaining the study purpose, procedures, risks, benefits, and their rights as participants.

2. Consent questions: Participants must answer questions confirming they understand the study and their rights before proceeding.

3. Electronic consent: Participants provide consent by clicking "Yes, I consent to participate" after reading the consent form. They can also click on an "opt out" button and exit the study without penalty.

4. Right to withdraw: The consent form clearly states that participants can withdraw at any time without penalty.

5. Contact information: The consent form includes contact information for the principal investigator and IRB for questions or concerns.

The consent form is stored electronically in the PRAMS system as part of the research record.
data_monitoring_plan: Data will be monitored throughout the study period to ensure data quality, appropriate participant recruitment, and compliance with IRB protocols. The principal investigator will review data collection progress and address any issues that arise.
oversight_procedures: Study oversight will be provided by the Principal Investigator and IRB. Any adverse events or protocol deviations will be reported to the IRB immediately.
publication_plan: Results from this study may be prepared for publication. All publications will use aggregated, anonymized data only.
data_sharing_plan: Individual participant data will be shared but in anonymized form. Aggregated data may be shared through publication and open science repositories.
participant_access_to_results: Participants who are interested in learning about the study results can contact the principal investigator to request a summary of findings.
appendices_notes: Supporting documents include consent form, assessment materials, debriefing materials, and recruitment materials. All materials are available for IRB review upon request.
irb_contact_notes: For questions or concerns about this research, participants can contact the Principal Investigator or IRB Office (Dr. Alaina Daigle, 985-448-4697).
